steps.title: Identify the chronological finding
steps.content: The excavations found iron workshops in Oboui and Gbatoro dated to 2300-1900 BCE—contemporary with Anatolia's 2200-2000 BCE. This simultaneous emergence of iron production in distant regions is what the correct answer must explain.

steps.title: Evaluate which explanation fits
steps.content: Choice D is the only option explaining how Central Africa could have developed iron contemporary with Anatolia: independent emergence in both places, not transmission from a single source. Options A, B, and C all require some form of Anatolian diffusion (revising its timeline, assuming an alternate route, or splitting the evidence), which becomes unnecessary if both regions independently produced iron at nearly the same time. That matches choice D—D is correct.
